title: Stellan Yarrow
content:
Stellan Yarrow (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as head coach of the Lunaris Comets when they won the coastal league championship in 1998.

Professional career.

Stellan Yarrow joined the Lunaris Comets and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1993s. By 1997, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Championship season.

The 1998 coastal league championship campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Stellan Yarrow averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Tallmere Memorial Arena in Tallmere.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the coastal league championship, Stellan Yarrow remained with the Lunaris Comets for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Early life and college career.

Stellan Yarrow played college basketball in Tallmere before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as head coach.

Legacy.

Historians of the Lunaris Comets cite Stellan Yarrow's 1998 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Tallmere Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Stellan Yarrow recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1998 playoffs.

Awards and honors.

Following the coastal league championship, Stellan Yarrow received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Playing style.

As head coach, Stellan Yarrow preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Lunaris Comets switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Personal life.

Stellan Yarrow retired to Tallmere and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 2000.
